title: Adana Kebab

seo.title: Authentic Adana Kebab Recipe: Taste of Turkey at Home
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of making authentic Adana Kebab with our easy-to-follow recipe. Get juicy, spicy Turkish skewers perfect for lunch or dinner. Impress your guests!
longTailKeywords: how to make Adana Kebab at home
Adana Kebab step by step guide
seo.h1: Authentic Adana Kebab Recipe: A Taste of Turkey
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Adana Kebab skewers with grilled vegetables and flatbread on a platter.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, spicy flavors of homemade Adana Kebab, a true Turkish delight perfect for any meal.

description: Adana Kebab is a succulent, hand-minced meat kebab from the city of Adana in Turkey, renowned for its spicy flavor and unique texture. Made with lamb or a mix of lamb and beef, seasoned with red pepper flakes and sumac, it's traditionally cooked on wide skewers over an open charcoal grill. This iconic dish offers a fiery, aromatic experience that captures the essence of Turkish culinary heritage, often served with lavash bread, fresh vegetables, and a squeeze of lemon.

ingredients.notes: coarsely minced, or a mix of lamb and beef
finely grated and squeezed of excess liquid
kuyruk yagi, finely minced, optional for juiciness

instructions.title: Prepare the Meat Mixture
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the coarsely ground lamb (and lamb fat if using). Add the finely chopped red and green bell peppers, grated and squeezed red onion, minced garlic, red pepper paste, sweet paprika, hot red pepper flakes, cumin, salt, and black pepper.

instructions.title: Knead Thoroughly
instructions.text: With wet hands, knead the mixture vigorously for at least 10-15 minutes until it becomes sticky and cohesive, like a paste. This develops the protein and prevents the meat from falling off the skewers.

instructions.title: Chill the Mixture
instructions.text: Cover the bowl and refrigerate the meat mixture for at least 2-4 hours, or preferably overnight. This allows the flavors to meld and the meat to firm up, making it easier to skewer.

instructions.title: Shape the Kebabs
instructions.text: Divide the chilled meat mixture into 8-10 equal portions. Take a wide, flat metal skewer and firmly press one portion of the meat around it, shaping it into an elongated, flattened oval, about 1 cm thick. Ensure the meat adheres well to the skewer.

instructions.title: Grill the Kebabs
instructions.text: Preheat your grill (charcoal for best results, or a gas grill to medium-high heat). Place the skewers directly over the heat. Grill for about 3-5 minutes per side, turning frequently, until the kebabs are nicely charred and cooked through but still juicy.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the kebabs from the grill. Let them rest for a couple of minutes before gently sliding them off the skewers onto a serving platter. Serve immediately with lavash bread, sliced onions with sumac, grilled tomatoes and peppers, and a fresh salad. Enjoy your homemade Adana Kebab!

tips: For authentic texture, use coarsely ground lamb or mince it yourself. Kneading the meat mixture extensively is crucial for binding and texture.
If you don't have wide flat skewers, you can shape the meat into patties and grill them, or use two thin skewers parallel for stability.
Serve Adana Kebab piping hot with warm lavash, a simple onion and sumac salad, and grilled long green peppers and tomatoes for a complete Turkish experience.
article: Adana Kebab is a beloved Turkish Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
